name: Trickster Gods
description: Divine troublemakers and shapeshifters who use cunning over brute force. These deities blur the lines between chaos and creation.

name: Rulers of the Dead
description: Sovereigns of the afterlife who guide souls, judge the deceased, and guard the boundaries between life and death.

name: Sky Fathers
description: Supreme rulers who command thunder, lightning, and cosmic order. These father figures often head their pantheons.

name: Gods of Love
description: Deities who govern passion, desire, beauty, and the bonds between hearts. Their influence shapes mortal and divine relationships alike.

name: Divine Warriors
description: Gods and goddesses of battle, strategy, and martial prowess. Some embody brutal warfare, others disciplined combat.

name: Children of the Sun
description: Radiant deities who drive the sun across the sky, bringing light, warmth, and the cycle of days.

name: Lords of the Deep
description: Rulers of oceans, rivers, and all waters. They command storms at sea and the creatures of the deep.

name: Keepers of Wisdom
description: Deities of knowledge, learning, arts, and civilization. They gift humanity with writing, medicine, and sacred mysteries.

name: In the Beginning
description: Stories of how the cosmos came to be - from primordial chaos to ordered creation, featuring the first gods and the birth of worlds.

name: The Great Floods
description: Cataclysmic deluges that washed away the old world. These universal stories explore divine judgment and humanity's resilience.

name: Divine Mothers
description: Nurturing earth mothers and protective maternal figures who embody fertility, nature, and the cycles of life.

name: The Forge Gods
description: Master smiths and artisans who create divine weapons, magical artifacts, and wonders of craftsmanship.
